grammar_points.title: 养成 — To cultivate / To form a habit
grammar_points.explanation: 养成 (yǎng chéng) means 'to form' or 'to cultivate', and is almost always used with 习惯 (xíguàn — habit) to mean 'to form a habit'. Structure: Subject + 养成 + 习惯/behavior. The pattern 养成...的习惯 (to form the habit of...) is extremely common. Example: 他养成了早起的习惯。(He formed the habit of getting up early.) Note: 养成 is a resultative verb compound: 养 (to raise/cultivate) + 成 (successfully become).

examples.chinese: 她养成了每天散步的习惯，非常积极。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǎng chéng le měi tiān sàn bù de xí guàn, fēi cháng jī jí.
examples.english: She has formed the habit of walking every day — very positive.

examples.chinese: 从小养成好习惯，长大后往往更成功。
examples.pinyin: Cóng xiǎo yǎng chéng hǎo xí guàn, zhǎng dà hòu wǎng wǎng gèng chéng gōng.
examples.english: Forming good habits from childhood often leads to greater success in adulthood.

exercises.correct: 他从小就养成了按时睡觉的习惯。
options: 他从小就养成了按时睡觉的习惯。

Tā cóng xiǎo jiù xí guàn le yǎng chéng àn shí shuì jiào.
Yǎng chéng cóng xiǎo tā, àn shí shuì jiào de xí guàn.
Tā yǎng chéng le cóng xiǎo àn shí shuì jiào.

grammar_points.title: 积累 — To accumulate gradually
grammar_points.explanation: 积累 (jī lěi) means 'to accumulate' or 'to build up gradually over time'. It implies a slow, consistent process of adding up. It can refer to experience, knowledge, habits, wealth, skills, or any other resource. Structure: Subject + 积累 + Object. Often used with time expressions to emphasize the gradual nature of accumulation. Example: 通过多年的积累，他的知识很丰富。(Through years of accumulation, his knowledge is very rich.)

examples.chinese: 他每天坚持散步，积累了很多好的生活习惯。
examples.pinyin: Tā měi tiān jiān chí sàn bù, jī lěi le hěn duō hǎo de shēng huó xí guàn.
examples.english: He persists in walking every day and has accumulated many good life habits.

examples.chinese: 平时多积累经验，才能发挥出最好的状态。
examples.pinyin: Píng shí duō jī lěi jīng yàn, cái néng fā huī chū zuì hǎo de zhuàng tài.
examples.english: Accumulate more experience in ordinary times, so that you can show your best.

grammar_points.title: 往往 — Often / Frequently (habitual tendency)
grammar_points.explanation: 往往 (wǎng wǎng) means 'often' or 'frequently' and specifically indicates a habitual or predictable tendency — something that regularly happens as a pattern. It refers to what usually happens in a given situation and implies a correlation or tendency. It is not the same as 经常 (jīngcháng — often, frequently in a general sense). 往往 implies 'often in such cases' or 'tend to'. Structure: Subject + 往往 + Verb/Adjective. Example: 坚持锻炼的人往往身体很好。(People who persist in exercising tend to be very healthy.)

examples.chinese: 养成好习惯的人往往比别人更积极。
examples.pinyin: Yǎng chéng hǎo xí guàn de rén wǎng wǎng bǐ bié rén gèng jī jí.
examples.english: People who form good habits tend to be more positive than others.

examples.chinese: 平时不注意健康的人，往往在生病后才后悔。
examples.pinyin: Píng shí bú zhù yì jiàn kāng de rén, wǎng wǎng zài shēng bìng hòu cái hòu huǐ.
examples.english: People who don't pay attention to health in ordinary times tend to regret it only after falling ill.

options: 改变习惯往往需要很多时间。

Gǎi biàn xí guàn xū yào wǎng wǎng hěn duō shí jiān.

culture_notes.title: Chinese Health Preservation Culture
culture_notes.content: 养生 (yǎngshēng — health preservation or nurturing life) is a deeply rooted concept in Chinese culture that goes beyond simply avoiding illness. It encompasses a holistic approach to living: maintaining a balanced diet, following regular routines, exercising moderately, and managing one's emotions. Traditional Chinese health wisdom includes: getting up and going to bed early (早睡早起, zǎo shuì zǎo qǐ), eating warm foods rather than cold, taking regular walks especially after meals, avoiding overwork, and practicing breathing exercises or Taichi. Many Chinese parks are filled with elderly people every morning doing group exercises, dancing, or playing badminton — a reflection of this culture. The concept of 养生 values gradual accumulation (积累) over time rather than quick results.

idioms.pinyin: shēng mìng zài yú yùn dòng
idioms.english: Life lies in movement (exercise is the key to life)

quiz.question: What does the phrase 养成习惯 mean?

What does '养成好习惯' mean?
Fill in: 他每天 _____ 散步，积累了很多健康经验。

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 小文，你平时都不坚持锻炼，这样下去不健康。
lines.pinyin: Xiǎo Wén, nǐ píng shí dōu bù jiān chí duàn liàn, zhè yàng xià qù bú jiàn kāng.
lines.english: Xiaowen, you usually don't persist in exercising. This is not healthy in the long run.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 妈，我知道，但是我往往太累，没有时间活动。
lines.pinyin: Mā, wǒ zhī dào, dàn shì wǒ wǎng wǎng tài lèi, méi yǒu shí jiān huó dòng.
lines.english: Mom, I know, but I tend to be too tired and don't have time to exercise.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 从今天开始养成散步的习惯，偶尔放松一下，减少压力。
lines.pinyin: Cóng jīn tiān kāi shǐ yǎng chéng sàn bù de xí guàn, ǒu ěr fàng sōng yí xià, jiǎn shǎo yā lì.
lines.english: Start forming the habit of walking from today, occasionally relax, and reduce stress.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 好的。我会积极去试试，不过能积累成一个好习惯吗？
lines.pinyin: Hǎo de. Wǒ huì jī jí qù shì shì, bú guò néng jī lěi chéng yí gè hǎo xí guàn ma?
lines.english: Okay. I'll actively try it — but can it really be accumulated into a good habit?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 当然！每天坚持，慢慢积累，习惯就会养成了。
lines.pinyin: Dāng rán! Měi tiān jiān chí, màn màn jī lěi, xí guàn jiù huì yǎng chéng le.
lines.english: Of course! Persist every day, accumulate slowly, and the habit will form.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 我明白了，改变生活方式需要时间，但我会努力的。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ míng bái le, gǎi biàn shēng huó fāng shì xū yào shí jiān, dàn wǒ huì nǔ lì de.
lines.english: I understand. Changing one's lifestyle takes time, but I will work hard.

lines.speaker: A
lines.pinyin: Jiā Miù! Nǐ yě lái gōng yuán sàn bù?
lines.english: Camus! You're here for a walk in the park too?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 是啊，我养成这个习惯已经五年了。平时早上七点就来活动。
lines.pinyin: Shì a, wǒ yǎng chéng zhè ge xí guàn yǐ jīng wǔ nián le. Píng shí zǎo shàng qī diǎn jiù lái huó dòng.
lines.english: Yes, I've had this habit for five years already. Usually I come to exercise at 7 in the morning.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 你往往来得很早！我偶尔来一次，你是天天来。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ wǎng wǎng lái de hěn zǎo! Wǒ ǒu ěr lái yí cì, nǐ shì tiān tiān lái.
lines.english: You tend to come very early! I only come occasionally once in a while, but you come every day.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 坚持才能保持健康，这是我多年的体会。
lines.pinyin: Jiān chí cái néng bǎo chí jiàn kāng, zhè shì wǒ duō nián de tǐ huì.
lines.english: Persistence is key to maintaining health. That's what I've learned over many years.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 你说得对。我也要改变生活方式，减少熬夜，多出来活动。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ shuō de duì. Wǒ yě yào gǎi biàn shēng huó fāng shì, jiǎn shǎo áo yè, duō chū lái huó dòng.
lines.english: You're right. I also need to change my lifestyle — reduce staying up late and exercise more in outdoors.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 好！积极地生活，健康地活动，这就是养生的秘诀！
lines.pinyin: Hǎo! jī jí de shēng huó, jiàn kāng de huó dòng, zhè jiù shì yǎng shēng de mì jué!
lines.english: Great! Living actively and exercising healthily — that is the secret of 养生!
